channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-27
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. In **scienceverse/metacheck**, the `dev` branch has been updated to flag p-values reported as exactly zero and rewritten OSF prereg extraction using a schema-driven approach. Meanwhile, **giladfeldman/docpluck** has resolved specific issues related to tables, including DP-3 and DP-5, with a focus on verification and version 2.4.98. **Davidvandijcke/coarse** has made notable updates, including localizing the confirmation email and review page, as well as enabling RLS on the schema_migrations bookkeeping table. Additionally, **JamieCummins/regcheck** has improved local reporting in the `staging` branch, demonstrating the community's commitment to enhancing research tools. These updates highlight the connections between projects, with a shared focus on improving research workflows and data analysis.

35 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: escicheck, auto-zcurve

Most active:

docpluck15 commits
metacheck10 commits
regcheck7 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: regcheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The `staging` branch was worked on, where the commit message specifically mentions that local reporting was improved. The change made was to "improve local reporting", as stated in the commit message, which was applied to the `staging` branch. No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The commit made by Jamie Cummins on 2026-06-26 targeted the local reporting aspect of the repository. This single commit is the only change made since the last update, focusing solely on enhancing the local reporting functionality in the `staging` branch.

1 commit(s) today:

  • fdfb805 [staging] improve local reporting — Jamie Cummins
  • 31fa103 [staging] improve multistudy ingestion — Jamie Cummins
  • b0d8ab2 [staging] debug of CLI report — Jamie Cummins
  • 97e8203 [staging] add further local LLM support in CLI — Jamie Cummins
  • df873ec [staging] UI fixes — Jamie Cummins
  • 5b53ad6 [staging] fix OSF + mutlistudy bugs — Jamie Cummins
  • 71be3be [staging] harden security features — Jamie Cummins
  • fbd5030 [staging] firm up qwen formatting — Jamie Cummins
  • f775856 [staging] improve qwen — Jamie Cummins
  • 724d020 [staging] re-add qwen! — Jamie Cummins
  • 89a1470 [staging] push for redeploy — Jamie Cummins
  • baebc81 [staging] hunting down osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 2f5eaaa [staging] improve logging — Jamie Cummins
  • 7fb80fe [staging] improve error message — Jamie Cummins
  • a2f7302 [staging] fix osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • d2089d5 [staging] remove groq endpoints — Jamie Cummins
  • 51b9ba6 [staging] harden json handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 6cdde08 [staging] fix oss bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 1fd2de7 [staging] use more efficient storage — Jamie Cummins
  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: activity update
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that synchronized changes from the `escicheckapp` repository at commit `f37b5a1`, which corresponds to `effectcheck` version `0.6.6`, as stated in the commit message "sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6)". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. The commit was related to issue `#20`. This commit brought the `main` branch in line with the specified version of `effectcheck`. The `main` branch now reflects the changes made in `escicheckapp` up to the specified commit.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: auto-zcurve: activity update
item.description:
shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve

The `main` branch was worked on, where the validation note was revised and a citation was included, as stated in the commit message "Revise validation note and include citation". No other branches were modified in this set of commits. This specific change involved updating the validation note to presumably enhance its accuracy or credibility by adding a citation. The revision of the validation note and addition of the citation were the sole changes made in this commit. There were no additions or modifications of features, fixes, or modules beyond the revision and citation addition. The work was limited to a single commit on the `main` branch.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
